description: LLM Council routing. Models don't compete — they compose. Each task gets the best model for that specific job.

source_discovery_twitter.why: Only frontier model with native live X/Twitter data access

significance_scoring.why: Classification task. Sonnet is 5x cheaper than Opus and equally good at scoring against a rubric

brain_connection_classification.why: Classifying relationship types (BRIDGE/CONTRADICTS/EXTENDS/CONFIRMS) is structured work

conviction_formation.why: Forming original takes requires deep reasoning and nuance. Opus only.

briefing_writing.why: Opus 4.7 has better instruction following (new tokenizer pays closer attention per word) — critical for following newsletter style rules precisely. Keep temp 0.6 for factual accuracy.

post_writing.why: Opus 4.7 for better voice-rule compliance. Higher temp (0.8 vs 0.6) for personality and variation — posts need creative energy, not safe output.

format_selection.why: Routing decision based on story type. Structured, fast.

hook_extraction.why: Finding the most surprising line in a draft. Pattern matching, not creative.

review_structure.why: Structural QA: hook strength, flow, format compliance

review_fact_check.why: Google Search grounding for real-time claim verification

review_concept_accuracy.why: Are the technical explanations correct? Would an ML engineer find errors?

review_teaching_quality.why: Could someone with no ML background follow this? Are there logic leaps?

review_voice_match.why: Comparing draft against voice profile requires nuanced judgment

review_adversarial.why: Is this take actually novel? Search X for similar takes. Lowest hallucination rate (4.22%).

review_freshness.why: Is this news actually new? Has someone made this exact connection already?

scoring_synthesis.why: Synthesizing all review outputs into a final confidence score
